# stream-fork

> A 1→N stream combinator — a Writable that duplicates every chunk to multiple downstream Writables, propagating backpressure from the slowest downstream. Part of the `stream-chain` / `stream-json` family. Zero runtime dependencies.

The whole point is correct backpressure: `Fork._write` only signals "ready for the next chunk" once every downstream has called back, so the slowest fork gates the upstream pipe. Without that, a slow downstream's buffer would grow unboundedly.

## Quick start

Install:

```bash
npm i stream-fork
```

Usage:

```js
const Fork = require('stream-fork');
const fs = require('node:fs');
const zlib = require('node:zlib');

const gzip = zlib.createGzip();
gzip.pipe(fs.createWriteStream('log.txt.gz'));

// push data to both the gzip chain and stdout
const forkStream = new Fork([gzip, process.stdout], {});
dataSource.pipe(forkStream);
```

## API

### `new Fork(outputs[, options])`

- `outputs` — array of downstream Writables.
- `options` — Writable options. Default `{objectMode: true}`. Opt out by passing `{}` for chunk mode.
  - `options.ignoreErrors` — boolean. When truthy, downstream errors are silently dropped and the failing stream is removed from `outputs`. When falsy (default), the first downstream error is re-emitted on the `Fork`.

### `Fork.fork(outputs[, options])`

Static factory. Equivalent to `new Fork(...)`. Lets you write `dataSource.pipe(fork([s1, s2]))` without `new`.

### `fork.outputs`

The array of downstream Writables. Mutated in place when a downstream errors out (the failing stream is filtered out).

### `fork.isEmpty()`

Returns `true` if `outputs.length === 0`. Useful for detecting that every downstream has errored out in default error mode.

## Common patterns

### Fan out to multiple sinks

```js
const Fork = require('stream-fork');

source.pipe(new Fork([sinkA, sinkB, sinkC]));
```

### Continue past downstream failures

```js
const Fork = require('stream-fork');

source.pipe(new Fork([primarySink, flakyMetricsSink], {ignoreErrors: true}));
```

### Fan out with per-output transforms

Compose externally using `stream-chain`:

```js
const Fork = require('stream-fork');
const chain = require('stream-chain');

source.pipe(
  new Fork([
    chain([compress, fileSink]),
    chain([stringify, networkSink])
  ])
);
```

## Notable changes in 2.x

- Node 22+ required.
- Repackaged under `src/index.js` with hand-written `.d.ts` sidecar; old `main.js` entry point removed.
- Test runner moved from `heya-unit` to `tape-six`.

## Links

- Repository: https://github.com/uhop/stream-fork
- npm: https://www.npmjs.com/package/stream-fork
- Full LLM reference: https://raw.githubusercontent.com/uhop/stream-fork/master/llms-full.txt
